content.text:
{For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible.}{Colossians 1:16}
 {In mathematics the art of proposing a question must be held of higher value than solving it.}{— Georg Cantor}

A **basis** is a minimal spanning set—a set of vectors that 
spans the entire space with no redundancy.

content.title: Basis
content.content:
A **basis** for a vector space $V$ is a set 
$\mathcal{B} = \{\vect{b}_1, \ldots, \vect{b}_n\}$ that:

1. Spans $V$: every vector in $V$ is a linear combination of 
 $\vect{b}_1, \ldots, \vect{b}_n$.

2. Is linearly independent.

content.title: Standard bases
content.problem:
1. The **standard basis** for $\R^n$ is 
 $\{\vect{e}_1, \ldots, \vect{e}_n\}$ where $\vect{e}_i$ has a 1 
 in position $i$ and zeros elsewhere.

2. The standard basis for $\mathcal{P}_n$ is $\{1, x, x^2, \ldots, x^n\}$.

3. The standard basis for $M_{2 \times 2}$ consists of the four 
 matrices with one entry 1 and all others 0.

content.title: Unique Representation
content.content:
If $\mathcal{B} = \{\vect{b}_1, \ldots, \vect{b}_n\}$ is a basis for 
$V$, then every vector $\vect{v} \in V$ can be written **uniquely** as
\[
\vect{v} = c_1\vect{b}_1 + c_2\vect{b}_2 + \cdots + c_n\vect{b}_n
\]
The scalars $c_1, \ldots, c_n$ are called the **coordinates** of 
$\vect{v}$ relative to $\mathcal{B}$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: All Bases Have the Same Size
content.content:
If a vector space $V$ has a finite basis, then all bases for $V$ have 
the same number of elements.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Dimension
content.content:
The **dimension** of a vector space $V$, denoted 
$\dim(V)$, is the number of vectors in any basis for $V$.

If $V = \{\vect{0}\}$, we define $\dim(V) = 0$.

content.title: Basis Theorem
content.content:
Let $V$ be a $p$-dimensional vector space. Then:

1. Any linearly independent set of exactly $p$ vectors is a basis.

2. Any spanning set of exactly $p$ vectors is a basis.

3. Any linearly independent set can be extended to a basis.

4. Any spanning set can be reduced to a basis.
content.label: Theorem

marginNotes.text:
He has made everything beautiful 
in its time.
